questions.question: Which country signed the Artemis Accords at NASA Headquarters in April 2026?
questions.explanation: Jordan recently signed the Artemis Accords at NASA Headquarters, expanding global participation in space governance. The Artemis Accords were launched in 2020 as a non-binding framework for guiding civil space exploration. It was co-led by NASA and the US Department of State along with founding nations like Australia, Japan, and the UK. India is also a signatory, highlighting its role in global space cooperation. The accords promote peaceful exploration and responsible use of outer space.

questions.question: What is the theme of Mehar Baba Competition-3 launched by Indian Air Force?
questions.explanation: The Indian Air Force launched Mehar Baba Competition-3 (MBC-3) on 27 April 2026 to promote drone-based radar innovation. It is the third edition of the competition, originally started in 2018, to encourage defence technology development. The theme is “Collaborative Drone-Based Surveillance Radars.” The aim is to develop a swarm of Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) acting as airborne radar for detecting and tracking aerial targets. The competition is named after Mehar Singh, a Maha Vir Chakra awardee and pioneering air veteran.

questions.question: Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor has been developed by which organization?

options.label: C
options.text: Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research

answer.label: C
answer.text: Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research

questions.explanation: India achieved a milestone with a 500 MWe (Megawatt Electric) Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam attaining first criticality on April 6, 2026. Developed by Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research and built by BHAVINI. It marks Stage-II of India’s 3-stage nuclear programme using uranium-plutonium mixed oxide fuel. Fast Breeder Reactors produce more fuel than they consume. India will become the 2nd country after Russia to operate commercial Fast Breeder Reactors.

questions.question: Where was National Deliberative Summit on “Harmonizing Administrative Data for Governance” held?
questions.explanation: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation hosted a National Deliberative Summit on “Harmonizing Administrative Data for Governance” in Bhubaneswar. The summit follows the Fifth National Conference of Chief Secretaries (2025). It aims to improve evidence-based policymaking and service delivery using administrative data. States and Union Territories assessed readiness through consultations.

questions.question: Operation Global-Hunt, that was recently seen in news, is mainly related to which one of the following?

options.label: B
options.text: Drug trafficking and transnational crime control

answer.label: B
answer.text: Drug trafficking and transnational crime control

questions.explanation: Notorious drug trafficker Mohammad Salim Dola was brought to India from Istanbul under Operation Global-Hunt. Operation Global-Hunt is related to drug trafficking and international law enforcement. It is a specialized operation conducted by India’s Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) in coordination with international and Indian intelligence agencies to track down, detain, and repatriate fugitives involved in major drug trafficking syndicates.

questions.question: Which countries hosted the First Conference on Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels in April 2026?
questions.explanation: The First Conference on Transitioning Away from Fossil Fuels hosted by Colombia and Netherlands in Santa Marta. It was announced in 2025 during COP30 under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change. The conference serves as a political platform to promote implementation-driven action for phasing out fossil fuels. It aims to support an orderly global energy transition toward clean energy.

questions.question: Which state government launched the Artificial Intelligence Policy in April 2026?
questions.explanation: The Government of Maharashtra launched the Artificial Intelligence Policy 2026 to boost investment, innovation, and employment. The policy aims to attract ₹10,000 crore investment and create over 1.5 lakh jobs. It includes the establishment of 6 AI Centres of Excellence and 5 AI Innovation Cities. The cabinet meeting was chaired by Devendra Fadnavis in Mumbai.

questions.question: Platypus species, that was recently seen in news, is a semi-aquatic mammal endemic to which country?
questions.explanation: Scientists discovered new fossils of an ancient platypus species in South Australia showing it had teeth, unlike modern platypuses. The Platypus is a semi-aquatic, egg-laying mammal endemic to eastern Australia. It belongs to a rare group of mammals called monotremes (egg-laying mammals). It inhabits freshwater systems ranging from tropical Queensland to Tasmania and the Australian Alps. It spends much of its time in burrows along riverbanks. Its conservation status is Near Threatened (IUCN).

questions.question: Where was National Security Summit 2.0 held?
questions.explanation: The National Security Summit 2.0 held in New Delhi aimed to discuss evolving national security challenges and strategies in the changing global order. The summit focused on strengthening India’s security preparedness, governance adaptation, and strategic response mechanisms. It highlighted that Operation Sindoor reflects India’s firm commitment to a zero-tolerance policy toward terrorism. It emphasised that India will make no distinction between terrorists and their sponsors in its security approach. The summit stressed the importance of adapting to the New World Order, especially in the context of global conflicts like West Asia.

questions.question: International Labour Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: International Labour Day, also called International Workers’ Day, is celebrated every year on May 1 to honour the hard work and contributions of workers across all sectors. The day highlights the need for fair wages, safe working conditions, and reasonable working hours for all employees. It also reminds us of the struggle of workers in history and the continued importance of protecting their rights. The International Labour Organization (ILO), a United Nations agency, plays a key role in setting global labour standards.
